purpose: Bootstrap module for the reverse-engineering and binary-analysis toolkit; installs apt tools, radare2 .deb, frida via pipx, a headless DOSBox-X wrapper, and Ghidra from GitHub releases.
invariants: radare2 is installed from the official GitHub .deb because it is not in Debian trixie apt
Ghidra requires java to be present (from the languages group) — skips with a warning if absent
reverse_dosbox_headless generates a wrapper that forces SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy for headless operation
reverse_record_manifest only records tools that are actually present after installation
gotchas: RADARE2_SHA256 and GHIDRA_SHA256 are intentionally empty — must be pinned by the operator after verifying the download
Ghidra download is ~400 MB; slow on metered connections
The dosbox-x-headless wrapper embeds a hardcoded path to ~/tools/etc/dosbox-x-headless.conf
GUI tools (Ghidra GUI, ImHex, Wireshark GUI) are explicitly Windows-native-preferred; only CLI/headless paths are installed here
reverse_ghidra detects symlink presence at ~/tools/bin/ghidra, not via command -v, to avoid false negatives on a fresh PATH

key_functions.name: reverse_radare2
key_functions.summary: Downloads the official radare2 .deb from GitHub, verifies SHA256, and installs via dpkg

key_functions.name: reverse_dosbox_headless
key_functions.summary: Writes a minimal headless DOSBox-X config and a wrapper script that forces the SDL dummy video driver

key_functions.name: reverse_ghidra
key_functions.summary: Downloads the Ghidra zip from NSA GitHub releases, extracts to ~/tools/ghidra, and symlinks ghidra and ghidra-headless into ~/tools/bin
